nlgyhjh.text: Hey thanks, appreciate the curiosity! I built Comforto using + Supabase for the backend and auth, moshi for audio agents and llm model training. On the app side, it’s a React Native / Expo build (so I could ship to both iOS and Android later), with a light Firebase layer for analytics and push. Costs are actually minimal right now: • Hosting + Supabase → under $20/month • voice agents- upto $3 for 30 minutes of interaction. • Everything else (frontend hosting, analytics, etc.) is free or hobby-tier.
nlgyhjh.parent_text: Sounds interesting. What was the tech stack you used to build it? What were the building and ongoing costs needed to support it for the next 6, 12, and 24 months? Just curious, as I have an idea but never get past 10 to 50% up and out.

A free plug from a space with a huge following will help get your product to increase usage.

nlhow10.text: No it was the issue with ui. Actually the app is designed to be used only on phones. But the app was allowed to install on iPad. Also point to note that they do install on iPad and check thoroughly. So if someone planning to release iPhone only app then make sure you disable iPad to false in the plist. The other reason is the payment wasn’t integrated properly with revenue cat. After fixing both issues finally they approved the release.
nlhow10.parent_text: What were the 2 rejection reasons? Was it something to do with audio / ai related issues or listing metadata issues?

nllwfro.parent_text: Congrats on the launch! Couple tips on App Store Optimization that will help with growth: Utilize all the space in your app name. These keywords hold the most weight and Apple will prioritize them when formulating keyword phrases Create organic keyword phrases that normal people would use. Nothing against your current subtitle, but my assumption is no one is searching for any of those words except for “support”. Use a keyword analysis tool like Apptweak to get a sense on the right keywords to target. Caption your screenshots. You don’t need anything fancy, but it will help potential users understand the core value promise that you are delivering on. Also, double dipping on keywords you use in your title and subtitle will further compound their impact. Best of luck out there! And keep launching :)

nlg6gti.text: That’s a good question actually! I wanted to go with real phone calls because the fake call screen trick doesn’t always hold up in real situations — people can notice the screen or timing feels off. A real call gives you that authentic ring tone, vibration, and interaction with your carrier’s call UI — it just feels real. Plus, since it’s an actual voice line, you can pick up, talk, and it sounds completely natural to anyone nearby. So yeah, the goal was to make it feel believable, not staged.
nlg6gti.parent_text: One query: why keep a phone call, instead you could keep fake call screen with same voice overs

It really acts as a kind of “call breaker” for awkward moments or when I need an excuse to exit gracefully. Thanks for creating something so practical!

nnqupte.text: For me, it’s the perfect tool to simulate fake calls—like when I’m in a meeting and need a smooth way to leave, or if I know I’ll have to step out after 5 or 10 minutes for something important. It really acts as a kind of “call breaker” for awkward moments or when I need an excuse to exit gracefully. Thanks for creating something so practical!

nlhvsuv.text: Congrats on the launch, Chetan. I launchedSoundSafealmost a year ago, a similar safety companion app for women that rings you an actual phone call to get out of uncomfortable situations. Looking back, it was hard to admit that the app wasn't getting many users. Still, seeing it go live and get approved on the App Store was an unreal feeling. Wish you luck and many users!
